step_by_step_thinking:
The presence of coarse facial features, macrognathia (large tongue), thickened lips along with hepatosplenomegaly suggest an underlying genetic condition that affects multiple organ systems. Among the given conditions - Hurlers Syndrome typically presents with corneal clouding which this patient does not exhibit; Beckwite-Weidman syndrome can present similarly but without the characteristic liver involvement seen here; hypothyroidism usually has distinctive neurological symptoms like developmental delay or myxedema which do not apply to this case; proteus syndrome often shows hemihyperplasia as one of its cardinal signs, though it may involve visceral organs such as the spleen. Given these findings, the diagnosis likely points towards Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome due to its multi-systemic presentation including macrosomia, abdominal wall defects, renal anomalies and overgrowth involving viscera.
